message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My task: add strip_metadata param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. I need to edit src/PIL/ImageOps.py and Tests/test_imageops.py. If your feature touches ImageOps.py too, let's coordinate on non-overlapping functions/sections.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My task: add transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. Only need ImageOps.py + test_imageops.py. Please propose your files if different.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both must touch exif_transpose in the same file/function - true disjoint files isn't possible here. Plan: you go first, add strip_metadata param + its tests, then run 'git add -A && git commit -m wip-agent1' so your change is committed. Then ping me DONE. I'll then add transform_callback on top as a separate uncommitted change, and my 'git diff' will show only my delta. I'll add my new param after yours in the signature (keyword-only, after strip_metadata) to avoid touching your lines. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Our features both touch exif_transpose in the same file, so full file-disjointness isn't possible. I'll keep my change minimal: add strip_metadata kwarg-only param to the signature/overloads, and a small block near the end of the function body (after orientation/exif handling, before the final returns) that clears image_exif.info when strip_metadata=True. I won't touch the transpose/method-application logic. Please add your transform_callback param+logic in the method-application block; as long as we each add our own kwarg to the signature without deleting the other's, edits should merge fine. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed. I'll add transform_callback as a keyword-only param after your strip_metadata in the signature/overloads, and apply it only in the method-selection block (the 'method = {...}.get(orientation)' line and its usage), leaving your info/exif-stripping block untouched. Will keep edits scoped so our diffs merge cleanly. Proceeding now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement strip_metadata now, save my diff to patch.txt, then git commit as a checkpoint (wip-agent1), then send DONE. You add transform_callback as the next kwarg after strip_metadata on top of that commit.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Heads up: I already implemented and tested transform_callback (added as a kwarg in the signature/both overloads, right after in_place, with the mapping logic replaced by 'if transform_callback is not None: method = transform_callback(orientation) else: method = {...}.get(orientation)', and method application generalized to a for-loop over methods to support sequences). I did NOT touch the exif/info-stripping block after 'exif_image = image if in_place else transposed_image'. Please add strip_metadata as another kwarg (after transform_callback is fine) and your logic in that untouched block. I have NOT committed - go ahead and edit on top of the current working tree; just avoid replacing the whole function body wholesale so we don't clobber each other. Ping me DONE when finished and I'll do a final test run.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: strip_metadata implemented, tested (4/4 passing, full test_imageops.py 35/35 passing), and committed as 6a462fb90 'wip-agent1: add strip_metadata to exif_transpose'. Signature is now: exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, strip_metadata=False). Please add transform_callback as the next kwarg after strip_metadata. My patch.txt is saved at /workspace/repo/patch.txt (will be overwritten by your final diff at the end - please regenerate your own patch.txt scoped to just your delta before you finish, e.g. git diff 6a462fb90 -- src/PIL/ImageOps.py Tests/test_imageops.py). Good luck!

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: transform_callback implemented and tested (36/36 tests passing in test_imageops.py, added 2 new tests). Turns out we're each in isolated sandboxes (your commit 6a462fb90 doesn't exist in my repo history), so no literal file clobbering risk - each of us just needs our own clean diff for our own feature. My patch.txt is written with only my ImageOps.py/test_imageops.py delta for transform_callback. Good luck with strip_metadata!
